metadata.title: Why four agents scoring the same bug beats one strong reviewer
metadata.topic: Consensus scoring as a confidence oracle
metadata.purpose: Each agent scores 0–100 independently; requiring inter-agent agreement (≥80) turns redundancy into a confidence measurement — issues where agents disagree fall below the line and are auto-suppressed.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: CLAUDE.md guideline checking, CI integration, taxonomy of false positives
metadata.visual_object: A scoring table — four agent columns, three issue rows, an average column, and a threshold line at 80 splitting posted from suppressed.
metadata.hook: Four independent agents rate the same issue with different confidence — and disagreement is the signal, not the noise.

beats.narration_text: Agent 1 flags "missing null check" (75), Agent 2 flags it (90), Agents 3 and 4 don't. The system averages to 82.5, clears the 80 threshold, and posts — one agent was sure, two doubted, consensus narrowly includes it.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If one expert review should be enough, why does splitting the review across four agents and averaging their scores separate true bugs from false alarms better than any single agent?
Four independent agents rate the same issue with different confidence — and disagreement is the signal, not the noise.
Each agent scores 0–100 independently; requiring inter-agent agreement (≥80) turns redundancy into a confidence measurement — issues where agents disagree fall below the line and are auto-suppressed.
beats.visual: A scoring table — four agent columns, three issue rows, an average column, and a threshold line at 80 splitting posted from suppressed.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Issue A [75,90,70,85]→80 posted; Issue B [50,45,60,55]→52.5 filtered; Issue C [95,92,98,96]→95 posted.
Each agent scores 0–100 independently; requiring inter-agent agreement (≥80) turns redundancy into a confidence measurement — issues where agents disagree fall below the line and are auto-suppressed.
